text:
From: ops@brightroot.com
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Jun 2029 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: High 5xx rate and throughput collapse across eu-us peering

Avery —

We're seeing a sharp increase in 5xxs and a 60-80% throughput drop starting ~07:55 PDT. Traces point at the VPC peering path to your eu-west transit (AS4455) where packets are being retransmitted and responses delayed. We also saw DNS timeouts where our resolvers report TTLs of 1s and a flood of A queries.

Attached: broot-debug.zip (pcap + traces + app logs)

Immediate impact: checkout flow failures for ~30% of users in US-east. We're hearing from product that this is hitting checkout conversions and potentially crossing our SLA.

Can you confirm whether any firewall rule/action, peering change, or DNS config rollou...

- Week 2 (Nov 15–19): Scenario modeling (base / conservative / upside) + cost levers.

Subject: Deadline breach: cordon & recovery plan for ACME evaluation

Hi Priya —

Quick update from last night's acceptance run: the final eval set hit a regression in multi-turn context handling that causes the acceptance criteria to fail intermittently. We ran a focused repro overnight and confirmed the root cause is related to a tokenizer edge-case when system prompts contain the customer metadata header.

Given that this directly impacts the close timeline, we recommend an immediate cordon (freeze) on any non-critical changes into the eval path and the following recovery steps:

- Cordon window: effective now until we confirm a stable acceptance run (expected 48–72 hours)
- Triage & o...

From: Priya Shah <priya@quantumretail.com>

Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@quantumretail.com>, Dan Moore <dan.moore@quantumretail.com>
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Clarify geo-failover SLA / RTO/RPO expectations

Hi Ben —

Quick question as we tighten up procurement: can Redwood confirm the SLA behavior during a full-region outage (e.g., us-east-1)? Specifically we need: 
- Target RTO for automatic failover (our internal target is <= 5 minutes)
- Target RPO (we'd like <= 15 minutes)
- Whether credits are applied for cross-region outages vs single-region degradation

Our security team is asking for the exact language that will go into the contract. If you have an ops diagram showing routing and cache warm-up assumptions that would help.

Thanks — Priya
